_comment: Source of truth for per-host xyOps hither-sync events. Read by register-hither-sync-events.sh (TODO). Each entry in 'hosts' becomes one xyOps event named 'hither-sync-<name>' under category 'AutoFS Share Map Sync'. Conductor on Umbridge schedules; xysat on each Mac runs hither-sync.sh locally — see admin-technical/conventions/synology-smb-and-mac-mount-architecture.mermaid for the data flow.
category_description: Daily per-Mac sync of /etc/hither_<nas> against the live share list visible to the target DSM user. Output is on-disk config + an automount reload; no other side effects. See admin-technical/conventions/synology-smb-and-mac-mount-strategy.md.
category_notes: SCOPE: Mac xysat workers ONLY. Canonical script: setup/synology/xyops/jobs/hither-sync.sh, fetched by each event's wrapper from Forgejo at a pinned commit SHA (Pattern #2). PREREQUISITES per host: (1) infra-agent NOPASSWD sudo for `tee /etc/hither_*` and `automount -cv` via /etc/sudoers.d/xysat-hither-sync; (2) 1Password item per NAS named '<nas> - DSM - <target_user> (Login)' in vault 'JRVIS Infra' containing the DSM password; (3) op CLI authenticated as the xysat run-as user; (4) /etc/auto_master entry for /Network/<nas> -> hither_<nas> -nosuid (one-time, per setup-autofs-<nas>.sh).
_failure_handling_note: catch_up: true so a Mac asleep at fire time defers to next reconnect. Script-execution failures (exit != 0) wired to ticket-on-failure with per-event source dedup — one open ticket per host's broken sync, separate from other hosts.
_error_run_events_note: emoqj8l7vyrzm942 = ticket-on-failure (same handler used by other Mac-xysat events).

morning-low-priority._comment: Daily, in the quiet 4 AM window — share lists change rarely so high cadence has no payoff.

_target_user_note: Each host syncs the share-list visible to the host's *primary* human DSM user — the one whose Finder mounts these maps drive. SusanBones=John's daily driver→johntrandall. Other hosts deferred until they have a daily-driver human user with their own DSM account; ChoChang/Curly (server roles, no Finder use) and Flitwick (Tart VM, ephemeral) are intentionally excluded for v1.

hosts.name: susanbones
hosts.note: John's daily driver. Drives /Network/umbridge/<share>. Add lupin/mcgonagall to nas_list once those NASes have analogous /etc/hither_* + sudoers in place.

excluded.name: chochang
excluded.reason: Server role (Tart VM host + Ollama). No interactive Finder workflow over SMB. Add only if a future workflow needs auto-mounted Umbridge shares for non-interactive use.

excluded.name: curly
excluded.reason: Bare-metal Tart VM host. Same reason as chochang.

excluded.name: flitwick
excluded.reason: Tart VM. Ephemeral state; no persistent SMB workflow yet.

excluded.name: umbridge
excluded.reason: It's the source-of-truth NAS, not a SMB client. xyOps Conductor on Umbridge schedules; xysat workers on Macs execute. Self-mounting from Umbridge to Umbridge would be circular.

_v2_open_questions: Add Lupin once it has shares warranting daily-driver mounts (currently mostly backup destinations).
Add McGonagall (Synology) — same DSM API path; just needs entry in nas_list and sudoers + auto_master setup.
Add Sprout (QNAP) — different API surface (QNAP REST). Separate worker function or a parallel script hither-sync-qnap.sh.

Multi-user Macs: today the script assumes one TARGET_USER per Mac. If a Mac genuinely has two human users with distinct DSM accounts and distinct Finder workflows, generate two events (different TARGET_USERs) writing distinct /etc/hither_<nas>_<user> maps. Defer until needed.
1Password access on xysat: confirm op CLI is authenticated as the xysat run-as user (infra-agent) on each Mac before scheduling. May require op service-account token in xysat env.
